fields.description:
# Plan-review verification gate (open→in_progress)

## Context

rebar has a completion-verifier *close* gate (verifies a ticket's work was actually done before close). It has
no equivalent at the *start* of work: when an agent claims a ticket (open→in_progress), nothing checks that the
ticket's implementation plan is sound before the agent executes it. Flawed plans (too big to one-shot, ambiguous,
wrong file targets, ignored consumers, missing tests, criteria that don't match the work, children that don't
cover the parent, unvalidated novel architecture) get executed anyway, and early errors compound over autonomous
agent trajectories (~44% of agent failures are spec/plan defects).

This epic builds a **plan-review verification gate** that fires at open→in_progress — the inverse of the
completion-verifier. It is a rebar FEATURE used by a BROAD, DIVERSE CLIENT BASE (many languages, frameworks,
project structures, and ticket-creation styles), so it must be polyglot, fail-open on unsupported stacks, and
flexible about inconsistently-scoped tickets. Its posture is to **COACH** agents to author better plans via
positive, well-formed guidance — not to nitpick or roadblock — while emitting a signed **certification** that a
review process was followed (a composable "rigorous agentic development vs vibe-coding" signal for CI).

Full design detail lives in session log `63cc-ec94-4792-464b` (plan-of-record v4 + the ~30 research reports +
the discussion narrative). This description is the self-contained epic summary.

## Design summary

- **Trigger / enforcement:** fires at open→in_progress. `claim` is a FAST signature-verify check only (no LLM, no
  network round-trip; target p95 < ~50ms). The heavy review runs OUT-OF-BAND via a dedicated entrypoint
  (`rebar review-plan <ticket>` / an MCP `run_workflow` tool, mirroring `rebar verify-completion`), triggered by
  the agent on a claim-block or by CI. A REVIEW PROCESS, not a dialog — the client revises and re-runs to earn a
  fresh signature (mirrors completion-verifier). Routing pivots on **has_children + complexity**, NOT ticket type.
- **Two layers:** LAYER 1 = a minimal, deterministic, structural floor that BLOCKS (frozen, unambiguous, cheap,
  polyglot-where-tooling-exists, fail-open; convergent by construction). LAYER 2 = an ADVISORY coaching review
  (per-criterion judgment + one bounded broad open-ended pass) that SURFACES findings authored for remediation
  and never blocks by default.
- **Verdict model:** per-criterion PASS / AMBIGUOUS / FAIL + a review-level INDETERMINATE (budget/tool-unavailable).
  Sound checks block; judgment is advisory; AMBIGUOUS escalates to a higher-tier adjudicator. A per-criterion
  config knob lets a project make a judgment criterion BLOCKING (= block-with-`--force`-override); INDETERMINATE
  is non-blocking by default (project-configurable).
- **Coaching / feedback spec:** the reviewer SUPPLIES the finding + location the agent can't self-detect
  (research: LLMs can't FIND their own errors but CAN fix them once located). Each advisory finding is grounded
  in a criterion/file/section, specific + actionable, severity-ranked + capped, affirms what already passes, a
  suggested fix only when confident, expressed as a checklist the agent addresses.
- **Criteria registry:** each criterion is a declarative descriptor carrying `exec` (DET | 1-TURN | 2-STEP |
  AGENT) + `facet` (the concern/context it examines) + `context_needs` + `routing` + `trigger`; a generic
  chunker reads these (reuse rebar.llm's catalog/contracts/prompt/runner) to bucket by tier and pack same-facet
  criteria into chunks — no hand-maintained chunk lists. Frozen within a review loop (convergence), grown between versions.
- **Polyglot tooling (fail-open IRONCLAD):** Layer-1 wraps external tools, ALL added as NEW integrations by this
  epic (none are wired into rebar today): LSP resolution via solidlsp (reachable through the repo's configured
  Serena MCP server, but NOT yet a rebar library) + ast-grep (resolution/callers); syft + per-ecosystem registry +
  LLM-fallback (deps); scc + lizard (size). Unsupported lang / no server / crash / timeout → skipped = PASS, never FAIL.
- **Execution (exec-tier-aware):** DET criteria run as parallel code (~free). SINGLE-TURN criteria are the only
  chunked tier — packed by `facet` at `base_chunk(model) × size_factor(ticket)` (config-overridable; default Opus
  ~all-in-one cached call, Sonnet 6 coherent / 3 plain, halved for large/epic/has_children). AGENT (tool-using)
  criteria run ONE agent each (≈85× a single-turn call) and are gated by proportionate scrutiny. Prompt caching on
  (system+plan prefix) makes fine chunking cheap. Depth scales with complexity + has_children; escalation by
  size + complexity + centrality (computed at plan time). Per-plan budget cap, chunk-atomic checkpointing;
  INDETERMINATE on cap-hit sheds AGENT/overlay criteria first. Defaults grounded by experiment (see below).
- **Attestation:** signed result = "process followed to the best of our ability, no red flags + coverage" — NOT
  "perfect". Bound to the latest MATERIAL event commit (description/AC/file_impact/decomposition edits invalidate;
  tags/comments/links/assignee do not). The cert is the composable CI rigor signal.
- **Observability / FP substrate (cheap, in-scope):** a reducer-IGNORED SIDECAR `REVIEW_RESULT` event on the
  ticket (out of hot paths) capturing per-criterion verdicts + finding fingerprints + metadata + override
  justifications; the signature stays a normal head-state event. Offline replay+join (ticket_id + fingerprints +
  existing EDIT/STATUS/completion-verification events) yields per-criterion fire/remediation/FP/FN rates. Capture
  only; analysis is offline; no human-feedback requirement.
- **Bugs are SKIPPED** in this epic (different shape; dedicated follow-on).

## Success Criteria

- [ ] Claiming a ticket (open→in_progress) requires a valid, non-stale signed plan-review attestation; absence or
      a material-edit-invalidated signature blocks the claim, with a recorded `--force` override available.
- [ ] The deterministic Layer-1 floor blocks only on sound, unambiguous checks and fails open on any unsupported
      language/tool (skipped = pass), with coverage recorded on the attestation.
- [ ] Layer-2 review returns coaching findings that are structurally well-formed — each carries criterion id,
      file/section location, severity, a checklist line, and a suggested fix when confident — verifiable in-session
      against the REVIEW_RESULT schema; judgment criteria are advisory by default. (Remediation-rate uplift is
      tracked OFFLINE as follow-on F1, not an in-session gate.)
- [ ] A project can configure risk posture, chunking, budget caps, and per-criterion blocking via config, with
      sensible defaults shipped.
- [ ] Every review emits a `REVIEW_RESULT` sidecar event sufficient to reconstruct per-criterion FP/remediation
      analysis offline, without taxing rebar's hot paths.
- [ ] The signed attestation is checkable by an external CI process as a rigor/anti-vibe-coding signal.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Layer-1 deterministic criteria implemented in Python, polyglot, fail-open: readiness shape (standardized
      `## Acceptance Criteria` across all types), file/symbol/import resolution (LSP/ast-grep), package existence
      (syft + registry + fallback), oversize signals, task-DAG validity, AC/DD quality, destructive sniff.
- [ ] Layer-2 advisory criteria implemented: measurability/agent-completable, user/problem present, criteria↔
      description coherence, ambiguity, intent fidelity (2-step blind-restate), testing-plan completeness,
      verification/termination, edit-set/scope accuracy (tool-primed), child coverage + consistency (containers),
      assumption/premise verification, anti-slop; plus triggered overlays (router-selected) and a bounded broad pass.
- [ ] Criteria registry (declarative descriptors) + generic orchestrator, reusing rebar.llm extension points.
- [ ] Attestation: signing + material-event-commit binding + invalidation-on-material-edit + coverage record,
      reusing the close-gate signing machinery (HMAC under the MCP environment key) — integrity/forgery/replay/
      staleness model inherited, no new key custody.
- [ ] `claim` integration: fast signed-attestation check with `--force`+justification override.
- [ ] Configurable chunking; proportionate-depth routing (complexity + has_children); escalation by
      size+complexity+centrality (centrality/blast-radius computed at plan time); per-plan budget cap with
      chunk-atomic checkpointing and an INDETERMINATE outcome (non-blocking default, configurable).
- [ ] Feedback-authoring conforms to the coaching spec (grounded/specific/ranked/affirming/checklist).
- [ ] `REVIEW_RESULT` reducer-ignored sidecar event with finding fingerprints; reducer skips the type; override
      logging; offline-analysis-ready.
- [ ] Per-criterion config: advisory (default) | blocking-with-override.
- [ ] Bugs are exempt from this gate (routed to a follow-on).
- [x] Defaults for chunking + budget + depth grounded in the experiment results (see `## Experiment-grounded defaults` below + docs/experiments/).
- [ ] Tests + verification: every AC maps to a concrete proving check; fail-open is tested per tool (unsupported-
      lang / no-server / crash / timeout → skipped=pass), plus tests for signature invalidation-on-material-edit,
      `--force` override logging, cap-hit INDETERMINATE shedding (agent/overlay-first), and the reducer-ignored
      REVIEW_RESULT sidecar (reducer skips the type; head/.cache/deps/validate unaffected).
- [ ] Docs: how the gate works, the config surface, fail-open behavior, the cert semantics, and the CI-check pattern.

## Experiment-grounded defaults (round 3; config-overridable)

Grounded by 5 experiment rounds (~1,600 LLM runs). Full design: docs/experiments/plan-review-criteria-storage-and-chunking.md.

- **Execution tiers are the primary axis (not chunk size).** DET (P1–P7) = parallel code, ~free. SINGLE-TURN
  (F1,F4,E2,E5,G5,T5a–e) = chunkable LLM calls, ~$0.005–0.008/criterion cached. 2-STEP (E1,E3,E6) = two ordered
  calls. AGENT/tool-using (E4,G1G2,A1,T1,T6–T8) = one agent loop per criterion, ~$0.12 + ~50s each (≈85× a
  single-turn call) — NOT chunkable; gated by proportionate scrutiny.
- **Criteria storage:** each descriptor declares `exec` (tier), `facet` (the concern/context it examines — the
  advisory grouping key), `context_needs`, `routing`, `trigger`. The chunker is generic: filter → bucket by tier
  → pack same-`facet` single-turn criteria up to the chunk size → one agent per AGENT criterion → DET as code.
  Groupings are advisory (facet tags re-pack at any chunk size); adding/tuning a criterion only declares exec+facet.
- **Chunk size = base_chunk(model) × size_factor(ticket), within the single-turn tier.** base_chunk = {opus 12,
  sonnet 6 with coherent facet packing (3 without), haiku/local 3}; size_factor = {trivial/moderate 1.0,
  large|epic|has_children 0.5}; clamp [2, n]. → Opus packs ~all single-turn criteria in one cached call (its
  consistency is flat in N); Sonnet 6 (coherent) / 3 (large); never the full set on the largest plans.
- **Enable prompt caching** (system+plan as the cached prefix): collapses the cost gap between chunk sizes (1.55×
  vs 2.6×), so chunk for reliability not cost. Cached fine-chunk Sonnet (~$0.07–0.09/review) ≈ one Opus pass
  (~$0.10) — prefer Opus for max consistency/big-plan stability, Sonnet+cache+coherent for the cost floor.
- **Budget (generous, per-plan):** cap = $0.15 (DET+single-turn base) + n_agentic_fired × $0.25 + headroom (small
  task ~$0.15–0.40; heavy epic ~$1.5–2.0 + minutes). INDETERMINATE on cap-hit sheds lowest-priority AGENT/overlay
  criteria first (85× cost), never the cheap base.
- **Criteria are fully specified from DSO source** (docs/experiments/data/dso-criteria-specs.md), not one-liners,
  and the set is >12 (single-turn ~13 + agentic + overlays + DET P1–P7).

## Self-review note (gate run on this epic, round 3)

The full gate was run on this epic per the default protocol (DET floor pass; Opus single-turn facet-chunks;
Sonnet agentic tier with real rebar-repo tools). It surfaced — and this revision addresses — the false 'already
in repo' tooling claim (E4/G1G2, caught only by the agentic tier), a missing test/verification AC (E5/E6), an
unnamed invocation surface (broad pass), an unmeasurable success criterion (F1), and a missing claim-path latency
target (T5a). The gate's **G5 finding is CORRECT and intentionally left open**: this epic should be decomposed —
the explicit NEXT action is to split it into the one-wave implementation plan (child tasks with deps). FP rate
was low (F4/E1/E2/E3/A1 passed); see docs/experiments/ for the baseline assessment.

## Follow-on work (to be filed as separate linked tickets, NOT part of this epic)

F1 FP/remediation-rate tracking tooling (offline analysis on REVIEW_RESULT) · F2 auto-demote blocking criteria on
FP ceiling · F3 confidence/severity floor for blocking · F4 promote checks to blocking-by-default as low-FP ones
are observed · F5 project configuration tool/UX · F6 bug-ticket review (dedicated epic) · F7 cross-ticket
no-overlap / dependency-aware semantic detection · F8 project-supplied criteria + project-invariant compliance ·
F9 code-drift invalidation · F10 calibrated abstention + labeled gold set · F11 rigor-signature chain / CI
integration helpers · F12 severity-minor-floor + NOT-flag tuning.
